themes: practical limits of measurement (mass, length, time)
ball bearing failure analysis (MH-53 helicopter / Stratford / Sikorsky)
DOD ownership transfer forms as standards
history of engineering as a profession
computer-aided design evolution (CAD → FEA → properties → manufacturability)
diamond vs. graphite as a case in chemical bonding and properties

major_cases.name: MH-53 helicopter swash plate bearing failure (Stratford crash)
major_cases.frame: Failure analysis case where bearing manufacturer was wrongly accused of producing improper-conformity bearings; Tom identifies barrel-shaped wear marks proving proper conformity, and traces actual cause to a potato-chipped aluminum swash plate housing milled on a defective machine the helicopter company scrapped.

major_cases.name: NIST visit and the platinum-iridium kilogram standard
major_cases.frame: Tom's visit to NIST and conversation with Katharine Gebbie about defining mass in terms of Planck's constant; introduces shipbuilding as the most precise large-scale manufacturing in parts-per-million.

major_cases.name: Diamond vs. graphite as bonding-determines-properties case
major_cases.frame: Same composition (carbon), drastically different properties from sp3 vs sp2 hybrid bonding; introduces graphene, Buckyballs, and nanotubes.
